revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:福井神社]][[simple:Fukui Shrine]]
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Fukui Shrine | location = Ōte 3-16-1, [[:en:Fukui City|Fukui City]], {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|en|Fukui Prefecture|qid=Q133879|lt=Fukui Prefecture|1=Fukui Prefecture}} | ISO = JP-18 | type = 36.066 | longitude = 136.219 | map = Japan Fukui | deity = Matsudaira Yoshinaga | founded = Shōwa 18 (1943) | type = Bekkaku Kanpeisha, Beppyo Shrine | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = {{ill|May 10|en|May 10|qid=Q2557|lt=May 10|1=May 10}} and {{ill|October 10|en|October 10|qid=Q2921|lt=October 10|1=October 10}} | image = File:Fukui Shrine Concrete Torii.jpg | location = {{ill|Fukui|qid=Q201618|1=Fukui (city)}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.066|N|136.219333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }}
Fukui Shrine (福井神社) is an important Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Fukui (city)|en|Fukui (city)|qid=Q201618|lt=Fukui city|1=Fukui (city)}}. It is classified as a Beppyo shrine, which means it is particularly notable in a certain way with a significant history to it. The shrine's main building was destroyed in {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}, but it has since been rebuilt using {{ill|Concrete|en|Concrete|qid=Q22657|lt=Concrete|1=Concrete}}. This makes the shrine quite unusual, especially with its striking concrete [[Torii]] gate.<ref>https://enjoy.pref.fukui.lg.jp/en/spot/spot-75/</ref>
Matsudaira Yoshinaga is enshrined at the Fukui Shrine.
==Overview== '''Fukui Shrine''' (福井神社, Fukui Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Fukui City|Fukui City]], {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|en|Fukui Prefecture|qid=Q133879|lt=Fukui Prefecture|1=Fukui Prefecture}}. It was formerly Ranked as a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Bekkaku Kanpeisha]].
== History == The shrine enshrines Matsudaira Yoshinaga, the 16th daimyo of the {{ill|Fukui Domain|en|Fukui Domain|qid=Q903537|lt=Fukui Domain|1=Fukui Domain}}, who implemented significant reforms in the domain and was a key pro-Imperial figure among the Tokugawa loyalists during the [[Meiji Restoration]]. It was established in Shōwa 18 (1943) to honor him, making it the last shrine designated as a Bekkaku Kanpeisha in Japan.
The original shrine buildings were constructed entirely of hinoki wood but were destroyed in an air raid during {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}} on July 19, Shōwa 20 (1945). The current shrine buildings were rebuilt in Shōwa 32 (1957) under the design of Igarashi Naoo from the [[:en:Fukui University|Fukui University]] Faculty of Engineering. The new buildings are constructed from reinforced concrete, featuring an innovative modernist architectural style with exposed concrete surfaces and a flat roof, which is a significant departure from traditional shrine architecture. The main torii (second torii), also designed by Igarashi Naoo, is unique in its lack of a middle crossbeam. In 2022, the shrine was recognized by [[DOCOMOMO JAPAN]] as a significant example of modernist architecture in Japan<ref>{{Cite web|title=Fukui Shrine |url=http://www.docomomojapan.com/structure/%e7%a6%8f%e4%ba%95%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ |website=docomomo |access-date=2022-06-08 |language=ja |last=DOCOMOMO}}</ref>.
The auxiliary shrine, Kodo Shrine, within the grounds, enshrines Bakumatsu-era Fukui samurai Nakane Yuki (雪江), Suzuki Chikara (純淵), and Hashimoto Sanai (景岳).
<gallery> image:Fukui-jinja honden.jpeg|main hall image:Fukui-jinja haiden.jpeg|Worship Hall image:Kodo-jinja in Fukui-jinja.jpeg|Auxiliary Shrine, Kodo Shrine </gallery>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{ill|Eiji Shirai|en|Eiji Shirai|qid=Q17216364|lt=Eiji Shirai|1=Eiji Shirai}} and Tsutomu Toki, editors. ''Jinja Jiten''. [[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]], 1979, p. 298.
== See Also == *[[Sakaenomiya Shrine]] {{Shinto-stub}}

If I get anywhere near implementing it, I will do so based on the actual completion tiers of this wiki rather than whether I added them when they were on simple english wikipedia or wherever--><!--Fukui Shrine--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Fukui-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11591715}} [[Category:1943 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Bekkaku Kanpeisha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukui (city)]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Japan completed in 1957]] [[Category:Facilities Opened in 1943]] [[Category:History of Fukui]] [[Category:Matsudaira Shungaku]] [[Category:Modern Movement Architecture in Japan]] [[Category:Modernist architecture in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukui Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
